COUNTERS AND A SCINTILLATION COUNTER IN
FROM BNL-325 AS 0.689 B AT 14.1 MEV.
.THIS WORK IS A CONTINUATION OF PREVIOUS STUDIES TO
INVESTIGATE THE MECHANISMS OF NUCLEAR REACTIONS AT
INTERMEDIATE ENERGIES, BOTH COMPOUND AND DIRECT.
.CORRECTIONS MADE FOR SMALL DIFFERENCES IN ANGULAR
DATA FROM TREATMENT OF DOUBLE DIFFERENTIAL
IN THE ENERGY SPECTRUM AT 0, 1.3, 2.4 AND
4 MEV AND INTEGRATING THE COUNTS IN EACH.
INTEGRATION OF THE ANGULAR DISTRIBUTION AT
.DATA OBTAINED BY PLOTTING THE ANGULAR DISTRIBUTION
FOR EACH OF THE ABOVE EXCITATION ENERGIES AND
ISOLATING AN ISOTROPIC PART AT BACKWARD ANGLES
ASSUMED TO BE DUE TO COMPOUND NUCLEAR EFFECTS.
